description_long: This sculpture depicts two life-sized male figures engaged in gold panning, a scene reminiscent of historical gold rushes. One figure is crouched, holding a pan and examining its contents, while the other stands, leaning forward with a pickaxe and offering a supportive hand. Both figures wear wide-brimmed hats and period-appropriate clothing, emphasizing their working-class status. The figures are mounted on a circular stone pedestal, which features a metal plaque on its side. The surface around the figures is textured to resemble rocky terrain, adding realism to the scene. The overall composition captures a moment of camaraderie and labor, with detailed attention to posture, clothing folds, and facial expressions.
description: A detailed sculpture of two men gold panning, mounted on a circular stone pedestal with a plaque. The figures are dressed in period attire and depicted in a dynamic, collaborative pose.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures clearly, with the crouched man holding a pan and the standing man offering his hand; the plaque on the pedestal is visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the standing man's extended arm and pickaxe, with the crouched figure's pan and the rocky terrain more visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the backs of both figures, with the stone pedestal and the figures' postures prominent.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the crouched figure's back and the standing figure's side, with the pan and rocky base in clear view.
attribute_subject_matter: two men gold panning during a historical gold rush
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is installed outdoors, likely in a public urban or park setting, as indicated by the stone pedestal and surrounding ground surface.
